vulnerabilities.title: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a Downstream Component in Codecov
vulnerabilities.description: Codecov npm module before 3.6.2 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via the "gcov-args" argument.

vulnerabilities.title: Command injection in codecov (npm package)
vulnerabilities.description:
### Impact

The `upload` method has a command injection vulnerability. Clients of the `codecov-node` library are unlikely to be aware of this, so they might unwittingly write code that contains a vulnerability.

A similar CVE was issued: [CVE-2020-7597](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-5q88-cjfq-g2mh), but the fix was incomplete. It only blocked `&`, and command injection is still possible using backticks instead to bypass the sanitizer.

We have written a [CodeQL](https://codeql.com) query, w

vulnerabilities.title: codecov NPM module allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands
vulnerabilities.description: codecov-node npm module before 3.6.5 allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands.The value provided as part of the gcov-root argument is executed by the exec function within lib/codecov.js. This vulnerability exists due to an incomplete fix of CVE-2020-7596.
